payload.goal: Gate readiness on database availability so pods are not marked ready when the database is unavailable, preventing traffic routing to unhealthy instances.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: Modify `HealthService.readiness()` so it returns false when `DatabaseHealth.isAvailable()` resolves to false and true only when both the process is ready and the database is available.
Keep readiness checks deterministic under DB errors by treating any `isAvailable()` rejection as readiness failure.
Preserve existing liveness and startup method signatures and semantics unless explicitly required by this contract, with no changes to their external contracts.
Add/extend `test/health-service.test.ts` cases to verify readiness becomes false when DB is unavailable and true when DB is available while process state is running.
Ensure the project still builds and tests via the existing `npm run build` and `npm test` commands and they both complete successfully.

protectedInvariants.statement: `HealthService` and `DatabaseHealth` public TypeScript types and method signatures remain source-compatible for existing callers.
No production dependencies are added and TypeScript strict-mode compliance is preserved under `tsconfig.json`.
Readiness is the only health gate changed for routing intent, with unrelated health semantics and deployment non-health settings unchanged.
`deployment.json` may be read for verification but readiness path (`/health/ready`) or existing probe definitions are changed only with explicit approval.

nonGoals: Implement broader routing or ingress/load-balancer logic outside the repository's health-service contract.
approvalRequiredChanges: Any edit to `deployment.json` readiness/liveness/startup probe parameters, paths, or behavior must be approved before merge.
evidenceGaps: There is currently no test that exercises the HTTP `/health/ready` endpoint flow end-to-end with the probe contract outside service unit methods.
risks: If `DatabaseHealth.isAvailable()` is slow or fails intermittently, readiness may flap and cause unnecessary traffic diversion.
Unexpected DB dependency in readiness can reduce availability during short transient outages unless probe thresholds remain calibrated for operational tolerance.
unknowns: Whether upstream infrastructure actually uses readiness probe failures from `/health/ready` as the sole traffic-routing signal is not provable from these files alone.
Whether any external gateway or service mesh bypasses Kubernetes readiness and routes traffic using additional logic is outside the inspected code surface.
